sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, text_column TEXT, num_column DOUBLE, date_column DATE);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'apple,banana,orange', 2.5, '2023-01-01'), (2, NULL, 5.0, '2023-02-01'), (3, '', 10.0, NULL);
SELECT string_to_array(text_column, ',') AS fruits, num_column * 2 AS doubled_value FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, string_to_array(text_column, ',') AS fruits FROM test_table WHERE num_column * 1.5 > 5.0;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS count, SUM(num_column) AS total FROM test_table GROUP BY string_to_array(text_column, ',') HAVING total > 3;

SELECT t1.id, t1.num_column * (SELECT AVG(num_column) FROM test_table) AS adjusted_value FROM test_table AS t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table AS t2 WHERE t2.id = t1.id AND t2.text_column IS NOT NULL);
SELECT string_to_array(text_column, ',') AS split_text, MAX(num_column) AS max_value FROM test_table GROUP BY split_text HAVING max_value IS NOT NULL;

SELECT id, num_column * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE text_column IS NOT NULL) AS multiplied_count FROM test_table WHERE num_column < (SELECT MAX(num_column) FROM test_table) AND text_column IS NOT NULL;

WITH FoundValues AS (SELECT string_to_array(text_column, ',') AS fruits, num_column FROM test_table WHERE num_column IS NOT NULL) SELECT f.fruits, SUM(f.num_column) * 3 AS multiplied_sum FROM FoundValues f GROUP BY f.fruits HAVING COUNT(f.fruits) >= 1;

SELECT text_column, num_column * 2 AS doubled, (SELECT string_to_array('x,y,z', ',') WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table WHERE num_column < 5.0)) AS conditionally_split FROM test_table WHERE date_column IS NOT NULL;
